Temporary exhibition 2026-2027 on the question of XQV0Z's urbanity between metropolis and countryside. From 11 February 2026 to 2 January 2027.
Terrain de pétanque en libre accès. Apporter ses boules. Cadre champêtre face au Parmelan.

Terrain forestier inégal, allées de terre potentiellement boueuses par temps de pluie. Bottes recommandées en cas de pluie. Accessibilité fauteuils roulants limitée. Petits groupes PMR bienvenus. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.
Terrain herbeux et plat, sentiers en terre. Accessible aux fauteuils roulants pour la zone pique-nique et l'aire de jeux principale. Tables et barbecue au sol. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.
The 20,000-object collection of the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z was assembled over a lifetime by Bernard Lacroix, a son of the village and a great collector of Alpine ethnology.
The 2026 temporary exhibition at the Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is "XQV1Z, town or countryside?", running from 11 February 2026 to 2 January 2027.

The 4,200 m² building now houses 700 m² of exhibition rooms across two levels, highly equipped to host international exhibitions (painting, sculpture, photography), the Charles-Ferdinand Ramuz media library spanning 700 m², and a congress space of 2,200 m² with a 382-seat auditorium. The building has recovered its central dome measuring 8.5 m in diameter reaching 35 m in height, its two stone campaniles adorned with flamed sandstone, and the murals by Jean Benderly in the vestibule.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z route is open 24/7 year-round (snow possible in winter). The booklet can be picked up during the opening hours of the valley's Tourist Offices. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z route is open 24/7 year-round. The booklet can be picked up during the opening hours of the valley's Tourist Offices. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is entirely free. No booking, no ticket: just pick up the booklet at the Tourist Office and play independently. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game starts at Le Buet SNCF station. Reach it by XQV1Z Express train from Chamonix or Saint-Gervais; by car via the Col des Montets, with free parking at the station.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game starts at XQV1Z SNCF station. Reach it by XQV2Z Express train (from Le Fayet or Chamonix) or by car via the A40 motorway, with free parking at the station.

The Au Fil du Rail trail game in XQV0Z is a free family quiz route created by the Chamonix Valley Tourist Office. 3 km and about 1 hr 30 of walking around Le Buet SNCF station, booklet in hand, to discover the village and its heritage.
The Boucles du Coteau in XQV0Z are open 24/7 year-round. Daytime is recommended to follow the waymarking and enjoy the views over Lake Geneva. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The Brévent Cable Car is open in summer (13 Jun–20 Sep 2026) 8:35–18:00 (8:20–18:00 Jul–Aug). Winter: 8:50, last descent 16:45–17:15. Closed 20 Apr–12 Jun 2026 for maintenance. Verified 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Bureau de la Montagne du Salève
The Calvary Ermitage Museum at XQV0Z is mainly open in July-August, Mon-Fri and Sunday 2 pm-6 pm, and during the European Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Cascade de Bérard via ferrata, at the hamlet of Le Buet in the Vallon de Bérard nature reserve, a playful route of bridges and walkways above the torrent and waterfall.
The Chavannes gondola: Single ride adult: €7.20, child: €5.40. MTB day pass adult: €34, child: €26. 6-ride card: €27.50. 12-ride card: €49. Free for under 5s. Verified on 9 May 2026.
The Chavannes gondola: Summer peak season (July–August): daily 9:00 – 17:30. Winter: approx. 9:00 – 17:00. Exact dates on lesgets.com. Verified on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Chavannes gondola: Yes, several establishments open in season: Le Chasse Montagne, La DZ, Le Vaffieu, Le Wetzet and the Restaurant du Lac, all with terraces and Alpine views.
The Château de Fonbonne lies in the historic La Touvière district of XQV0Z, in the town centre, near the Thermes. GPS: 46.400° N · 6.593° E.
The Château de Fonbonne stands on the site of a 14th-century fortified manor that defended the new district of La Touvière at XQV0Z.

The Château de XQV0Z was the seat of a châtellenie of the Counts of Geneva until 1401, when the county was sold to Savoy. Gradually abandoned, it was razed around 1630 by Louis XIII's royal troops during the War of the Mantuan Succession.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z costs €5 full price, €3 for young people, and is free for under-12s. Free entry on the first Sunday of the month (September-June). Verified 10 June 2026.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is at the Espace Carpano & Pons, 100 place du 11 Novembre, in the centre of XQV1Z (74300). Train station 500 m away.

The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is open Tue-Sat 2 pm-6 pm from September to June, and every day 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August. Annual closure 11-30 November. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z is open mainly from June to August: June and low season Tue-Sat 10 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm; July-August extended hours 10 am-12 pm and 3 pm-7/8 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Counts' Castle of Ternier site is open 24/7, year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the remains and enjoy the panorama. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The Crêt de Châtillon, highest point of XQV0Z, reaches 1699 m according to the Lake XQV1Z Tourist Office. Some sources indicate 1702 m. An orientation table at the summit identifies the surrounding massifs 360°.

The Curalla via ferrata hosts no organised events: it is a free-access mountain route. The Saint-Gervais, Chamonix and XQV0Z guide offices occasionally run collective via ferrata introduction outings, often coupling Curalla in the morning with a second via in the afternoon (Bérard, Saix de Miolène). The XQV1Z Tourist Office publishes a summer mountain activities programme including supervised introductions.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z costs €5 for adults and €3.50 for children. Group rates on request on +33 (0)4 50 72 39 68. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z is at the Domaine de Guidou in XQV1Z (74140), 10 min from XQV2Z. Address: 217 route du Moulin de la Glacière.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z is open in May, June and September on weekends 2 pm-6 pm, and every day (public holidays included) 10.30 am-7 pm in July-August. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Débaroule at XQV0Z is included in the summer Pass Découverte or accessible with a pedestrian pass for the Patinoire cable car. 2026 prices to be confirmed at forfait.laclusaz.com. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z runs during the summer season (mid-June/early July to end of August), depending on the summer lift calendar. Check forfait.laclusaz.com. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Débaroule is part of XQV0Z's summer Pass Découverte, which combines several activities on the domain (pedestrian lifts, attractions).

The Dévalkart is mainly open from June to September (subject to weather and resort opening). Booking is generally not required — tickets on site. Expect €5-8 per descent, with 5/10/day passes at lower per-descent rates. The site is at the XQV0Z / La Croix-Fry resort, accessible by car from XQV1Z via XQV2Z (~40 min) or from XQV3Z (~15 min). Resort parking, chairlift departure close to the Dévalkart. Combines well with a family day at the resort and a mountain restaurant lunch.
The Ferme de Chosal Trail is open from 5 April to 3 November, 10 am to 6 pm, with last entry at 4.30 pm (information verified on the official website on 10 June 2026, subject to seasonal changes).
The Follow the Fly trail starts from Place du Trophée, in the heart of the village of XQV0Z. It forms a 2 km loop with 12 stops through the lanes and along the Chéran.
The Grand Balcon Sud links Planpraz to Flégère along the Aiguilles Rouges crest at 2,000–2,500 m — one of the great Alpine traverses, accessed from Planpraz.
The Grands Montets is a benchmark freeride destination: Combe de la Pendant, Glacier des Rognons, Combe Grisette. Guide accompaniment is mandatory for glacier itineraries.

The Grands Montets is a winter ski destination (December–early May). It is completely closed throughout summer 2026 due to the new 3S cable car construction. Full reopening — including access to the 3,275 m summit — is planned for December 2026 according to Compagnie du XQV0Z. For summer 2026, visitors wanting a view over the Argentière glacier should consider the XQV1Z or Montenvers instead.

The Grands Montets is included in the Chamonix Le Pass: adult day pass 59.20–74 € depending on the period. An AlpiSki AGM ski touring ticket is also available at 36.80 € for adults (desk purchase only). Verified 28 April 2026.
The Granges de Servette Museum is at the hamlet of Collongette, 224 Chemin des Granges de Servette, 74140 XQV0Z, 25 min from Thonon and 20 min from Geneva.
The Granges de Servette Museum is open from 29 June to 31 August, every day 3 pm to 7 pm. Occasional openings during the Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Hameau du Père Noël, open since 1989 in neighbouring Saint-Blaise, is one of the oldest permanent Christmas attractions in Europe. A
The Herbularius garden at the Château de Fonbonne in XQV0Z is freely accessible, especially during the growing season (spring to autumn). Check ville-evian.fr. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne combines well with a visit to the Évian Thermes, the Palais Lumière or the Jardins de l'Eau du Pré Curieux.

The Jaillet Gondola opens summer 2026: 4 July–30 August. Exact hours to confirm on lesportesdumontblanc.fr — 2025 reference: 9:15 am–5:45 pm. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to change.
The Jaillet Gondola: Based on 2025 prices: adult return €19, child 5–14 €14.50, adult single €11.50, day pass €22.50. Under 5 free. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026.

The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail tells 20,000 years of geological history of the Vallée d'XQV0Z: great glaciers, slow retreat, peat bog formation, and the story of an intriguing erratic boulder left on site.

The Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z is open from 21 December 2025 to 31 October 2026, every day 2 pm-7 pm (except Saturdays from 1 January to 30 April). Annual closure 1 November to 20 December. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Mont Veyrier cable car was inaugurated on 29 July 1934 by banker Léon Laydernier. It transported 2 million passengers over 50 years, reaching 70,000 passengers in 1948. It held two world records: 1,525 m cable span and 808 m elevation gain without intermediate pylons. Closed in 1984 for economic reasons (cost of safety upgrades), the upper station was demolished in 2001 after a fatal accident in 1998.

The Mont-Chéry gondola: Single ride adult: €7.20, child: €5.40. Summit round-trip adult: €10, child: €7. Family pass (min. 4 people): adult €9, child €6.30. 6-ride card: €27.50. Free under 5. Verified 28 April 2026.
The Mont-Chéry gondola: Summer peak season (July–Aug): daily 9:00 – 17:30. Winter: approx. 9:00 – 16:45. Exact dates on lesgets.com. Verified 28 April 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z displays more than 20,000 objects of XQV1Z peasant life gathered by Bernard Lacroix: forge, bakery, cobbler's workshop, old-time grocery and tools.
The Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z is open mainly on Thursdays in July-August for guided tours, 3 pm-7 pm. Openings by appointment for groups. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Nature Detective booklet for the XQV0Z walking loop can be downloaded free of charge from the Syndicat du Vuache website (pays-du-vuache.fr), or picked up at the Monts du Genevois Tourist Office and at the town hall.
The Nature Detective trail in XQV0Z is open 24/7 year-round (except frost/snow). Daytime is preferable to read the panels. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Nature Detective walking loop in XQV0Z is entirely free: open-access trail, booklet downloadable for free from pays-du-vuache.fr. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Nature Museum at XQV0Z is made up of 4 spaces: naturalised mountain wildlife, a glass-fronted hive with 30,000 bees, a teaching garden of medicinal plants, and a conservation orchard.
The Nature Museum at XQV0Z is open to the general public during school holidays, and year-round by reservation for groups and schools. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is open Wednesday to Saturday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm. Closed Sunday-Tuesday and public holidays. Annual closures: 8-26 August and 23-26 December 2026. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is open year-round, every day on request. SMS booking required on +33 (0)7 62 21 88 68. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The PAYSALP Peasant Museum at XQV0Z is open Monday to Friday 10 am-12.30 pm and 1.30 pm-5 pm (until 6 pm from 10 July to 1 September). Verified 10 June 2026.

The Parc de l'Imperial is on avenue d'Albigny, a 10-minute walk from the Pâquier. By public transport: Sibra bus, Impérial stop. By car: paid underground car parks Hôtel de Ville and Impérial nearby. By bike, the Lake XQV0Z greenway runs along the foot of the park.

The Parc des Dronières surrounding the Dronières Lake offers an animal park with fallow deer, red deer and mouflons (feeding not allowed), the TactiQ tree-top adventure course, a 2.5 km fitness trail, a permanent orienteering course, and new playgrounds delivered in 2026.

The Pass'Beun is a day package at €18 giving unlimited access to all XQV0Z activities: swimming, canoe, pedalo, stand-up paddle, Télébeune (aquatic zip-line), Wibit (inflatable structures), trampolines, sports fields. Indiana Parc adventure course is not included.
The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum is free for children under 12. The adult price is to be confirmed with the XQV1Z Town Hall on +33 (0)4 50 02 97 76. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum is open Monday to Saturday with varying hours: Monday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Tuesday 9 am-12 pm, Wednesday 9 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm, Thu-Fri 9 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Saturday 9.30 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-6 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z is open every Tuesday in July and August, 2.30 pm-5.30 pm. Outside the summer season, opening by appointment on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Pléney Gondola runs 5 July–31 August 2026 daily 9:00 am–5:30 pm. Evening openings to 7:00 pm Tues & Thurs 8 July–28 August. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Pointe Percée Geological Trail starts at the Col des Annes (1721 m), in the commune of XQV0Z, and climbs up to the Pointe Percée refuge at around 2164 m, at the foot of the eponymous summit.
The Pré Curieux Water Gardens in Évian: outstanding water garden on the shore of Lake Geneva, accessed by shuttle boat. Guided tour, April-October.
The Rovorée manor and La Châtaignière host temporary exhibitions, artist residencies and cultural activities (variable programming).
The Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z (at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce) mainly offers guided tours on Friday at 3 pm during school holidays. Outside holidays, opening by appointment. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z exhibits around fifty objects (out of 600+ kept): 17th-18th-century Baroque polychrome sculptures, liturgical gold and silverware, paintings and embroidered priestly vestments.
The Secrets de fées booklet is sold for €5 either at the Alpes du XQV0Z Tourist Office (XQV1Z / Boëge) or directly at the Foyer nordique des Moises, at the start of the trails.

The Secrets de fées discovery trail has four themed routes at the Plateau des Moises: Les Sources Claires (Hydris, 1 h 30), Les Prés Fleuris (Florine, 1 h), Le Beau Pays (Bohême, 1 h 15) and Les Vertes Forêts (Sylvaine, 1 h 15).
The Secrets de fées trails have free access year-round. Only the discovery booklet with its accessories is paid: €5 at the Alpes du XQV0Z Tourist Office or at the Foyer des Moises.
The Super-XQV0Z Gondola runs 26 June–31 August 2026 daily. Exact times to confirm (2025: 9:00 am–5:15 pm). Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

The Tactiq Game is an outdoor adventure game in the style of an "escape forest" at the heart of the Dronières forest, blending riddles and observation. It costs €10 per person, in addition to or independently of the tree-top courses.

The Thiou, natural outflow of Lake XQV0Z, runs for 3.5 km through the old town to the Fier. Pont des Amours, canal du Vassé, Palais de l'Île, Sadi Carnot floodgates: XQV1Z's heritage backbone is best discovered on foot.
The Tour of Lac du Môle is open 24/7, year-round. Spring to autumn is preferable for bird-watching and walking comfort. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Tour of Lac du Môle walk is in XQV0Z (74250), at the entrance to the village of XQV1Z, at the foot of the Château de XQV2Z, in the XQV3Z region. GPS: 46.140° N · 6.420° E.
The Tour of XQV0Z Village map is available free at the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'XQV1Z tourist information office in XQV2Z itself.
The Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z is open year-round on Thursdays and Saturdays, 2 pm-6 pm. Guided tours on Saturdays at 10.30 am and 2.30 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z costs €4 adult, €3 child 4-16, €3.20 student, free under 4. Combined two-museum ticket: €7 adult / €5 child. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z opens mainly during school holidays: April Monday-Wednesday-Friday, and July-August Monday to Friday 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-4 pm (Thursday 2 pm-6 pm). Verified 10 June 2026.
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail is entirely free: the trail is in free access, and land-art creations using the natural materials on site are unlimited. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail is freely accessible 24/7, year-round. Spring to autumn is best for the light and for comfortable creating. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z are open daily from 15 March to 15 October, from 9:30 am to 6:15 pm in low season and until 7:15 pm in summer (15 June – 10 September). The ticket office closes 1 hour before the site. The site is closed from 16 October to 14 March.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z cannot be visited inside. The external view from the road is free. No ticketing. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z is visible from the road 24/7, year-round. No visiting hours (interior closed). Verified on 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z flows year-round, but the experience varies greatly by season. The flow is spectacular in spring (May–June) during snowmelt, with flow reaching several cubic metres per second and impressive water sprays — surroundings become slippery and dangerous. In summer (July–October), flow decreases but remains visible, direct car access and the site is very busy on weekends and in peak season. In autumn (September–October), visitor numbers drop, light is beautiful and surrounding forest displays colours. In winter (December–April), the road is closed after Salvagny; access is on foot or by snowshoe (1h round-trip), offering spectacular winter scenery with partially frozen waterfall and assured tranquility. Best light for photography: morning (9 am–11 am) or late afternoon, avoid noon (backlighting).

The XQV0Z formed on the night of 11–12 March 1943, during a landslide that blocked the course of the Brévon river. About ten chalets were swept away, and the submerged hamlet of L'Écondu now lies beneath the water.
The XQV0Z in XQV1Z is open continuously, 24/7, every day all year. The park is unfenced and does not close. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z in XQV1Z occupies the former 18th-century domain built by Joachim de la Grange, Count of XQV2Z, then the Clos du Chalet (1857) built by Laurent-Marie Humbert, a watchmaker who made his fortune in Paris.

The XQV0Z in XQV1Z sits on the site of the former châteaux of Count Joachim de la Grange (18th c.) and the Clos du Chalet (1857), demolished or transformed. The park keeps their memory and their views.
The XQV0Z is accessible on foot from central Chamonix (45 min walk from Grépon car park via the Chemin à Batioret) or in 10 minutes from the car park on the XQV1Z tunnel access road.

The XQV0Z is accessible year-round, but optimal conditions fall between April and November. In spring (April–June), flow is at its maximum thanks to snowmelt and the Pélerins Glacier — the spectacle is particularly impressive, with tumultuous water and powerful roaring. In summer (July–August), the waterfall offers a much-appreciated cool refuge on hot days, and the adjoining chalet-café is open for refreshment on the terrace. In autumn (September–October), the colours of the surrounding forests enhance the setting, and visitor numbers drop. In winter, access depends on snow conditions — the trail may be covered with ice in places and become difficult to traverse; the waterfall can even be partially frozen during cold snaps, forming ephemeral natural sculptures. For an optimal experience and favourable light for photography, choose a morning visit.

The XQV0Z is open April to mid-November. April, May, June, September, October: Friday, Saturday, Sunday and public holidays 1 pm to 6 pm (last entry 5:30 pm). July–August: daily 10 am to 7 pm (last entry 6:30 pm). Exceptional openings during Easter and All Saints' holidays: daily 1 pm–6 pm. Verified on the official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

The XQV0Z is open Tue-Sat 9.30 am-12 pm and 2 pm-5.30 pm out of season, and daily 9.30 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August (except Sunday morning). Annual closure 1 November-15 December. Verified 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z is open daily 10 April to 7 September 2026 from 10:00 AM to 6:30 PM (ticket office closes at 5:30 PM). 8 September to 4 October 2026: 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM (ticket office closes at 4:30 PM). Annual closure early October through mid-April. Verified on official website 6 June 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

The XQV0Z is open year-round, but the summer season (May to September) offers the most activities: supervised swimming in July and August, café-restaurant open mid-April to mid-September, ideal conditions for windsurfing and paddling. Spring and autumn are quieter, perfect for fishing and walking. In winter, the site remains accessible for lakeside walks, but water sports are suspended. Note: refurbishment works from September 2025 to June 2026 will limit access to swimming and fishing lakes.

The XQV0Z opens every day on a seasonal schedule: 7 am-10 pm in June, July and August; 7 am-8 pm in April, May, September and October; 7 am-7 pm from November to March (source: XQV1Z Tourisme listing, verified on 10 June 2026).

The XQV0Z site is still reachable on foot, but since 6 January 2026 a municipal order has closed the Bété trail. Only the climb from the Morsulaz car park is usable. Check the commune website before setting out.

The XQV0Z water sports base is a few minutes' walk from Évian town centre, from Évian SNCF station (XQV1Z Express to Geneva via XQV2Z) and from the quay car parks. CGN boats dock at the neighbouring landing stage.
The XQV0Z water sports base is on avenue du Général Dupas, on Évian beach by Lake Geneva, 10 minutes' walk from Évian SNCF station. GPS: 46.400° N · 6.583° E.

The XQV0Z water sports base is open in the summer season daily from 1 pm to 7 pm, with a late opening until 9 pm on Thursdays. Lifeguarded beach June-September 12 pm-6 pm (11 am-7 pm in July-August). Checked on 10 June 2026.

The activity is exclusively winter and depends on snow (typically December to March). Ride prices ~€50-80/person, kennel visits ~€10-15/person, longer outings on quote. Booking essential, several weeks ahead in high season (Christmas, February). Wear very warm clothes (thick coat, gloves, hat, waterproof warm boots). Cancellation possible in case of insufficient snow or extreme weather.

The admission price for the discovery course at XQV0Z is €14 for an adult and €13 for a child. The main course costs €20 for an adult and €18 for a child. Group rate (from 8 people): -€2 per entry. Verified on 14 May 2026.

The adult admission price (15+) for the guided tour of the XQV0Z is €12. Child (5–14): €6. Free for children under 5. Gardens and terraces only ticket: €3. Group rate (10+ people): €10 per person. Verified 14 May 2026.

The adult entrance fee for XQV0Z (from 16 years) is €9. Child (4–15 years): €6. Free under 4 years. Reduced rate: groups (15+ people on reservation), persons with disabilities, hikers who walked or cycled from the valley. 15% of ticket price funds 2027 operations (closure for rehabilitation). Verified 14 May 2026.

The adult price for the XQV0Z is €15. Children 6–18 years: €8.50. Free for children under 6. Family Pass (2 adults + 2 children): €38.50. Student (under 26): €11. Season pass (unlimited entry April–October): €25. Verified 6 June 2026.
The adult rate for XQV0Z is €7. Child 6–16 years: €3.50. Adult group (min. 20 persons): €5.60. Free for under 6 and with Multi Pass Portes du Soleil or Pass XQV1Z France. Verified May 14, 2026.

The adult rate for the Advanced Course at XQV0Z is €27. Junior Course €22. Children's Course €17. Acro'Ninja, Hop Up Challenge and Acro'Filet €17 each. Sensory Forest €5 (free under 3). Treasure Hunt €10. Orienteering €6. Multi-activity pass available. Group rates on request. Verified 5 June 2026.
The adult rate for the main course at XQV0Z is €26. Youth under 16: €20. Children's course: €12 (1 round) or €17 (2 rounds). Group rates from 9 people: €22/adult, €17/youth. Verified May 14, 2026.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is freely accessible from May to October depending on snow cover. Mid-mountain trail — avoid in snow or thunderstorms. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The beach is accessible year-round and free. The best period for swimming is June to September, with water reaching 20–24°C in July–August. During high season (27 June–31 August), the beach is paid and supervised from 10am to 7pm, with heavy crowds in sunny afternoons — prefer early morning or evening. Off-season (April–May, September–October), access is free, the atmosphere more peaceful, but water cooler (15–18°C). In winter and spring, the beach remains open for lakeside walks, but without supervision or swimming possible.

The beach is accessible year-round, but the ideal bathing season runs from June to September, with water reaching 22 to 25°C in July–August. Supervised swimming operates from 1 July to 30 August. Spring and autumn offer a quieter atmosphere, ideal for walks on the sand and observing dune flora. In winter, the beach becomes a popular birdwatching site, with the calm waters of the Vion stream hosting diverse lakeside wildlife. Summer events (concerts, sand festival, artisan market) are in full swing in July and August. Avoid busy weekends during hot periods in peak season for a more pleasant experience.

The best starting point to discover the Thiou is the Pont des Amours, at the outflow of Lake XQV0Z on the Pâquier side: from there you can head up the canal du Vassé and the Port arm through the old town.

The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is a stone dated 1579, set at the foot of the steps near the church of XQV0Z. It is today the only tangible remnant of the seigneurial Château du Vuache, which dominated the village in the Middle Ages.
The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is accessible 24/7, year-round, with free access. Daytime is preferable to read the 1579 date clearly. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The castle of XQV0Z was built in the 15th century by the marquises of XQV1Z in Gothic style, enlarged until the 17th century then acquired by the Gerbais de Sonnaz family in 1696. It was burnt down by the SS on the night of 25 to 26 December 1943, during the Christmas 1943 massacre that cost the lives of 32 people.

The centre is open year-round, with extended hours during school holidays. Public prices are around €5-8 for adults and €3-5 for children; 10-entry cards, monthly/annual passes and reduced rates are available. Swim caps compulsory, soapy shower before swimming. Free parking, bus access from Évian and Thonon. Perfect for an indoor activity alongside a day by the lake.
The centre is served by CCPEVA bus lines from Évian and Thonon. The Lake Geneva cycle greenway runs nearby — ideal by bike from Évian-Amphion.

The cinema joins national operations (Fête du Cinéma in late June/early July, Printemps du Cinéma in March, Rentrée du Cinéma in September) and hosts occasional previews, talks with directors and pop-up cine-clubs. Programming changes every Wednesday — see the official site.

The cinema joins national operations (Fête du Cinéma in late June/early July, Printemps du Cinéma in March, Rentrée du Cinéma in September) and hosts occasional previews, talks with directors and relays. Programming changes every Wednesday — see the official site.

The cinema takes part in national operations: Fête du Cinéma (June/July), Printemps du Cinéma (March), Rentrée du Cinéma (September). Previews, talkback screenings, family sessions and occasional film clubs throughout the year.

The cinema takes part in national operations: Fête du Cinéma (June/July), Printemps du Cinéma (March), Rentrée du Cinéma (September). Previews, talks with directors, family screenings, opera/concert relays.

The cirque is spectacular year-round, with very different atmospheres depending on the season. May–June is the peak period: over 30 waterfalls fed by snowmelt cascade down the cliffs with impressive flow, set among colourful vegetation. July–August: high visitor numbers, heat, fewer waterfalls (about a dozen persist). September–October: low crowds, autumn colours, snow-free peaks, ideal for wildlife watching. December–March: cross-country domain and snowshoeing, frozen waterfalls, monochrome landscape. Check the webcam before you go to verify conditions (haut-giffre.fr).

The cirque's rocks are Jurassic limestone, heavily fractured. High rainfall intensifies erosion: a major rockfall occurred in 1602 (burying several hamlets, commemorated by a chapel), then in 2003 (300,000 m³) and September 2025 (35,000 m³ at Pas Noir), leading to temporary closure of the Fond de la Combe sector by municipal order.

The classic route departs from Bois Brûlé parking (Villaz) via the Grand Montoir (1 h 45, equipped with cables) and descends via the Petit Montoir (easier). For a short access, depart from L'Anglettaz chalet (Aviernoz): 1 h 15 ascent, 382 m elevation gain.

The climb to the XQV0Z from Morsulaz is short but steep (significant average gradient). The route remains accessible to families used to mountain walking. The gentler Bété trail is currently closed by municipal order.

The col is generally open from early May to mid-November. In 2026, it opened on 6 May at 2 pm and will close around 18 November depending on snow conditions. Check inforoute74 for the current road state.

The concept suits families with children (selection from age 5-6) as well as friend groups, stag/hen, birthdays (children, teens, adults), after-work drinks and team-building. Access to the game library is a small flat fee (~€3-5/person) giving unlimited play all evening. Drinks (beer, wine, softs, snacks, boards) are extra.
The exterior view of the XQV0Z is freely accessible year-round, 24/7, from the road and the local paths. No scheduled interior visit. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The external view of the XQV0Z is free year-round. The CCBC offices inside the château are open on weekdays, but without tourist visits. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The external view of the XQV0Z is free. There is no regular tourist interior visit (CCBC headquarters). Verified on 10 June 2026.
The footpath alongside the via ferrata continues to the Pierre à Bérard refuge (2 h from the buvette), in the heart of the Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve.
The guided tour of the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z lasts about 1 hour, led by a volunteer of the Muséâm association.

The ideal season for Col des Pitons runs May through October, when the road is fully snow-cleared and accessible by car and bike. In winter (November–March), the road closes roughly 1.5 km before the pass (from Grotte du Diable onward). Cyclists favor June through September for climbs, with peak traffic on sunny weekends. Hikers prefer spring (May–June) and autumn (September–October) to avoid heat and enjoy autumn colors. Note: On July 7, 2026, the road is closed to cars in the morning (9am-12pm) for "XQV0Z Au Sommet" (cyclists only). Check the Department website before traveling that day.

The leisure complex is open daily in July and August, the busiest period with all activities running and permanent swimming supervision. In June, opening is limited to weekends and public holidays. The lake water, naturally temperate, is most pleasant from late June to late August. Outside high season, the complex offers afternoon opening from 1:30pm for unsupervised swimming and adventure course. The restaurant "À la Belle Étoile" remains accessible year-round Thursday to Sunday (except July-August when open daily), allowing you to enjoy the lake setting even outside summer. Autumn and spring offer a quieter setting for hikers wishing to discover the site without the summer crowds.

The original cable cars (Lognan I and II) were destroyed by fire in August 2018. A new 3S cable car designed by Renzo Piano has been under construction since 2024, linking Argentière to the 3,275 m summit. The Plan Joran gondola provides interim winter access.
The park is at Beunaz Beach: supervised swimming, paddle, canoe, pedalo, inflatable lake structures, Télébeune (zip line), diving boards.

The park is open year-round with hours varying by season. High season (April to August) offers the widest time slots with evening openings until 7:30 pm on weekends. In winter, the park operates Wednesdays and weekends with daily opening during school holidays. Spring and autumn are ideal periods to enjoy the forest with less crowding. Allow 1 to 2h30 depending on the course chosen. Avoid heavy rain or strong wind days. Online booking required, possible until the last minute.

The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z is walkable from around 1 May to 19 November, outside the snow season. In winter the upper section is snowy and used for cross-country skiing or snowshoeing depending on conditions.
The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z runs through the XQV1Z valley (AOP cheese country), follows the Dranse de Sous-le-Pas river, and allows you to reach the Maison du Fromage XQV2Z and XQV3Z Abbey.

The price for XQV0Z varies by course: Grand Course adult (16+) €26, youth (under 16) €21, Junior Course €20, Children's Course €16, Acro'Net €16, Orienteering course €6. Group rate from 8 people. Verified May 14, 2026.
The price of the Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is €3.70 full price, €3 reduced. FREE for everyone on Wednesdays and free all year for under-22s and students. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The project is run by the Office de tourisme des Alpes du XQV0Z and the Foyer nordique des Moises. It introduces families to mountain life past and present while encouraging respect for nature: alpine flora, streams, summer pastures, plateau landscapes and mixed forest. The trails suit family outings as much as gentle walks for adults, and can be tackled
The remains of the Château de XQV0Z are visible 24/7, free access year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the arrow slits and the curtain wall properly. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The road is closed in winter, usually from mid-November to early May depending on snow cover. In 2026 the col reopened on 6 May at 2pm. Access is free year-round during the open season, and a free shuttle bus runs to the col from XQV0Z from 11 July to 21 August.
The ruins of the Château de XQV0Z are open 24/7 year-round. Guided tours by the Heritage Guides are by reservation. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The season runs from June to late September depending on water levels and weather. Prices range from around €55-80 for a half-day to €85-110 for a full day (2026 indicative prices, to confirm on the official site). Booking is via escape-canyon.com or by phone, ideally several days ahead in peak season. The meeting point is usually in XQV0Z or near the chosen canyon, communicated when you book. Plan extra driving time to the canyon itself.

The season usually runs from June to late September depending on river flow. Prices are around €55-80 for a half-day and €85-110 for a full day (2026 indicative prices, to confirm on the official site). Booking is via neo-canyon.com or by phone; very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. The meeting point is usually in XQV0Z or close to the chosen canyon, communicated when you book.

The seven trails are graded by level: a children's trail (from age 3), then beginner, intermediate, sporty and expert routes. A safety briefing at the start is compulsory. Spectacular ziplines stretch up to 400 m above the thermal park, with sweeping views of the XQV0Z range, a unique setting for treetop adventures.
The single price for the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z is €5. Free for children under 6. Verified 10 June 2026.
The single price for the Nature Museum at XQV0Z is €6 per person, free for under-2s. Groups €12/person with activity. Family: 3rd person onwards €10. Verified 10 June 2026.
The single rate at the Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is €4 per person. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The site has been developed since 1869, when 250 m of footbridges were fixed into the rock 25 m above the torrent. Access to the footbridges is paid, and the trail is open from 15 March to 15 October. Around the site, the commune of XQV0Z has created the Chemin des Châteaux, a free 2 km walking loop linking the entrance of the Gorges to the Château de Montrottier via the pont des Liasses and the pont de Pontverre.

The site includes supervised swimming with a pool fed by lake water, diving boards of various heights, the famous Télébeune (aquatic zip-line inaugurated in 1959), an Indiana Parc tree-climbing course, water sports activities (canoe, pedalo, stand-up paddle), inflatable structures, trampolines, sports fields, as well as a restaurant and unusual accommodation (tree houses, tipis, yurts) to extend the experience.

The site is equipped for relaxation and outdoor sports: a 23-apparatus fitness trail between the second and third lakes, volleyball and pétanque courts, table tennis tables, equipped picnic areas, outdoor showers near the first aid post, public toilets and seasonal café-restaurant. A bird observatory at the northern end of the third lake enables observation of mallards, coots and wild rabbits.

The site is part of XQV0Z's Ville d'art et d'histoire label, with interpretive panels and a direct link to the Jardins de l'Europe. It is a natural stop between the old town, the lake and the avenue d'Albigny — and just a short walk from the Pont des Amours and the canals district.

The site is rich in history: a marshland island in the Middle Ages, the "Clos Lombard" was given to the Visitation order in 1644 by a Lombard banking family, became national property at the Revolution, then bought back by the town between 1835 and 1843. Free 24/7.

The site stands out for its exceptional biodiversity: it is home to 350 plant species, 87 bird species, 29 butterfly species and 13 bat species, including the pygmy pipistrelle (unique colony in XQV0Z). The domain is not an urban park but a preserved natural space, shaped over centuries by human practices. The Rovorée manor and La Châtaignière residence, two 19th-century manor houses, now host temporary exhibitions and artist residencies.

The site's main draw is the outdoor balneoform pool (open June-September) — the main reason to come in summer. The Spa is the winter option par excellence. Daily access from €25-32 depending on the offer.

The ski area is accessible in winter (6 Dec 2025–3 May 2026) via the Plan Joran gondola (skiers only). It is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for the new 3S cable car works. Full reopening is planned for December 2026.

The slot-machine room is open daily 10am–3am, while the table-game room opens every evening 9pm–3am. The combination of casino, palatial hotel and lakeside setting makes the Impérial Palace one of the landmark addresses of XQV0Z's belle-époque waterfront.

The summit features technical installations (mountain rescue radio relay, weather antennae) and a visitor logbook box. Hikers can reach the summit via two main variants: Épaule du XQV0Z (the most frequented route) or Panse (less-used route). The ascent passes through several remarkable points: XQV1Z refuge (2,002 m), Mont Géroux (2,288 m), and the arrival of the Épaule chairlift (closed in summer). On clear days, visibility extends to the most distant massifs, offering a unique reading of the Alpine relief. XQV2Z is also the culminating point of the Diagonale du Mont-Joly, a vertical kilometer race of 4 km and 1,000 m elevation gain, organized in summer and winter versions.

The summit of the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges offers a 360° panoramic view over the Massif des Bauges (geopark), the Bornes massif, the town of Faverges and the surrounding basin. The wooden gallery (hourd) allows for detailed landscape interpretation.

The village and ruins of the Château de XQV0Z (74270) are the remains of a former châtellenie of the Counts of Geneva. The castle was razed around 1630 by Louis XIII's troops. The site is freely accessible and can be visited on a guided tour.

The village of XQV0Z has two beaches: the main 'Sous les Prés' (400 m of lawn) and the smaller, more confidential 'La Pointe'. A snack-bar opens in season at Sous les Prés, along with paddle and pedalo hire. Swimming is not supervised, so parental vigilance is recommended for children.
The visit to the Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z must be booked by SMS on +33 (0)7 62 21 88 68, mentioning "Musée du Ski", at most 2 days before the visit. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The water sports centre at Espace Bernard Pellarin is open from 18 May to 30 August 2026. Public opening hours (outside school group periods in May-June): Monday to Friday 11:15–13:30 and 16:30–19:00, Wednesday and weekends extended hours. Verified on the CCPC website on 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Île de Tortuga is part of the national Tortuga network (Lille, Saint-Étienne, Paris 19, Grenoble), known for its

Thematic temporary exhibitions (2 to 3 per year): painting, sculpture, photography. Public guided tours daily at 14:30. Family visits Wednesdays at 16:00 and Sundays at 11:00. Educational workshops for children and teenagers (€5 per workshop). Lectures and occasional cultural events organized by the City of Évian's cultural services and the Maison des Arts du XQV0Z.
Thonon Agglomération bus lines. Évian or Thonon SNCF station nearby (~15-20 min).
Throughout the route, open view facing the XQV0Z massif: XQV1Z, Dôme du Goûter, Aiguilles de Chamonix, Aiguille Verte on clear days.

Throughout the year, Espace Le Bois aux Dames hosts concerts, shows and sporting events. In summer, festivals and outdoor events on the base: Les Pépites (live concerts), August 15 celebrations with concerts, torch-lit parade and sound and light shows. Detailed programme on the XQV0Z town hall and Tourist Office websites.
Tiralo mis à disposition par les sauveteurs, rampe PMR (pente 4 %) depuis la rue de la Plage et places de parking PMR.
Toboggans géants, ponts suspendus, labyrinthe, parcours d'aventure, mini-terrains de foot et de basket, tyrolienne, Power Tower.

Today, the Glières Plateau is a natural area classified Natura 2000 and a Sensitive Natural Area, appreciated year-round: hiking and alpine pastures in summer, cross-country skiing (36 km of trails) and snowshoeing in winter. Accessible via two mountain roads (from XQV0Z/XQV1Z to the west or Le Petit-Bornand-les-Glières to the east), the site offers an escape 45 minutes from XQV2Z. Six mountain restaurants serve Savoyard specialties and alpine cheeses, some accessible on skis in winter.
Toupe-Toupe pour les moins de 5 ans
Tour de la Reine · donjon de 30 m du XIIIe siècle
Tour du XQV0Z à vélo 38-42 km
Tour médiévale XIVe siècle Port de Rives
Tour médiévale XIVe siècle au Port de Rives de Thonon, dernier vestige d'une maison forte de la mestralie créée 1283, dont le nom évoque une redevance féodale unique abolie 1773.
Toute l'année (intérieure) + hiver extérieure
Toute l'année (selon météo)

Toute l'année : Lundi à Samedi 10h-12h30 et 14h-18h. Dimanche et jours fériés : APRÈS-MIDI UNIQUEMENT 14h-18h. Jeudi sur réservation obligatoire (places limitées). Premières 3 semaines d'août : visite musée + fonderie tous les jours 10h-12h30 et 14h-18h. Fermeture annuelle 25 décembre et 1er janvier. Dernière entrée 2 h avant fermeture. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

Toute l'année : Mercredi, Samedi, Dimanche 10h–19h. Vacances automne, hiver et jours fériés : Mardi au Dimanche 10h–19h. Vacances d'été du 1 juillet au 31 août : Mardi au Dimanche 10h–18h30 (horaires variables selon affluence). Ouvert tous les jours fériés. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.
Toutes les salles sont accessibles aux personnes en fauteuil roulant, y compris les sanitaires.
Toutes les salles sont entièrement accessibles aux personnes à mobilité réduite (par le bas des gradins).

Toutes salles du Muséum accessibles aux fauteuils roulants y compris les sanitaires. Gratuit moins de 5 ans. Pas de tarifs spécifiques étudiants/chômeurs/handicapés (entrée déjà modique 4 €). Vérifié le 23 mai 2026.
Towel and swimsuit sales and rental, goggles, armbands. Ski lockers in front of entrance (€6 per day, 4 pairs of skis or 2 sledges per locker).
Trail free. The book 'Alpage de mon enfance' by Guy Chatiliez is on sale at the XQV0Z town hall and the XQV1Z and XQV2Z Tourist Offices. Verified on 10 June 2026.
Trail included in the summer Pass Découverte or with a pedestrian pass for the Patinoire cable car. Exact 2026 prices to be confirmed at forfait.laclusaz.com. Verified 10 June 2026.
Trails partly accessible for people with reduced mobility. Vehicle access possible to La Châtaignière domain.

Train AR adulte 31,50 €, jeune/senior 26,80 €, Tribu 97,70 €. Pack Voyage Mer de Glace (train + télécabine + grotte + Glaciorium) adulte 49,70 €, jeune/senior 42,20 €, Tribu 148,30 €. ALPI Pass adulte 43,30 €. Balcon Nord adulte 48,40 €. Moins de 5 ans gratuit. PMR + accompagnant 50 %. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.
Tramway TANGO XQV0Z-Genève arrêt 'Hôtel de Ville' à proximité immédiate. Gare XQV1Z XQV2Z Express (Genève-Coppet-Évian-Saint-Gervais) à 10 min à pied. Bus TAC lignes urbaines XQV3Z Agglo.
Transfert du 'regât du Crôt' raccard 2026
Transfert et installation du 'regât du Crôt' (raccard, terme patois vallorcin) adjacent au musée principal, après scan 3D effectué en 2025. Nouvelle attraction patrimoniale pour l'été 2026.
Transports publics selon ligne saisonnière vers XQV0Z. XQV1Z Express + cars Région XQV2Z + XQV3Z Express selon zone. Vérifier horaires saisonniers.
Transports publics vers XQV0Z selon saison. XQV1Z Express + cars Région XQV2Z. Vérifier ligne et horaires sur monticket.haute-savoie.fr.
Transports publics vers XQV0Z selon saison. XQV1Z Express + cars Région XQV2Z. Vérifier ligne et horaires.
Traverser le glacier du Géant en télécabine

Tree-top adventure park in the heart of the Dronières forest at XQV0Z: three tiered courses (Discovery from 90 cm, Junior from 1.15 m, Vertige from 1.40 m), ziplines, monkey bridges and a Tarzan swing. Season runs from April to November.
Treetop adventure park at XQV0Z (74): 8 trails, 70 workshops, ziplines from age 3. Children's island €10, ages 6–16 €17, adult €23. May to October.
Trek mondial autour du massif du XQV0Z en 11 étapes anti-horaire, traversant France, Italie et Suisse sur 170 km classiques (215 km avec variantes XQV1Z TMB).

Trips run May to September, outside thunderstorms (water safety). Booking is mandatory, online or by phone, with weather confirmation the day before. Prices range from around €55 for a discovery half-day to €80-85 for a sporty full day (2026 prices to confirm). The meeting point is given at booking depending on the canyon. Canyoning is a physical activity: swimming and good general fitness are required. Instruction is provided by state-qualified instructors (DEJEPS or BEES Canyon).
Trois formules : Kids (15,90 €/enfant), Kids Premium (17,90 €), Kids Premium Plus (19,90 €). 2 h, 8 à 20 enfants, 3 adultes gratuits. Créneaux 10h-12h ou 14h-16h.
Tronçon de la Véloroute Vallée de l'Arve

Très peu. Le XQV0Z est un monument médiéval sur îlot rocheux avec étages exclusivement en escaliers. Rez-de-chaussée partiellement accessible. Accès uniquement par les passerelles piétonnes. Vue extérieure libre depuis les ponts.
Trésor caché
Tunnel creusé chaque hiver dans le glacier, accessible depuis la télécabine. Salles thématiques sur la vie d'alpiniste et l'évolution du glacier.
Two bars-restaurants on the base: L'Entre 2 Lacs and the Espace Le Bois aux Dames bar. Terraces with lake views.
Two descents in a row, or rafting + hydrospeed with picnic on site. Around €75-95 depending on format.

Two main routes: (1) From Les Praz de Chamonix, Flégère cable car to 1,877 m, then 2 hours' walking to XQV0Z. (2) From Tré-le-Champ car park at Col des Montets, 3 hours' hiking with technical passages (ladders). The Flégère option is more accessible.
Two-cable giant zip-line in XQV0Z: 1,200 m + 1,325 m, up to 240 m high and nearly 100 km/h. €43/person, open June to September.
Tyrolienne enfants (à vérifier sur place)
Tyrolienne pour enfants 5-15 ans mentionnée dans la documentation Loisirs 74 mais non confirmée par les sources tourisme officielles. Présence à vérifier sur place avant de planifier la visite.
Tyrolienne traversant la frontière France-Suisse au-dessus du torrent. Unique en Europe. Incluse dans les grands parcours.
Télébeune – aquatic zip-line
Télécabine 4 places en pulsé reliant le sommet de l'XQV0Z à la pointe Helbronner en 30 minutes, au-dessus du glacier du Géant, à la frontière France-Italie.
Télécabine de 30 min au-dessus du glacier du Géant jusqu'à la pointe Helbronner (3 466 m, Italie). Ouverture 4 juillet – 27 sept 2026. Passeport requis.

Une des trois guérites est la guérite Paul Perroud, préservée en l'état comme atelier in-situ d'un pêcheur du XQV0Z du milieu du 20e siècle. Outils, filets, moteurs, ambiance authentique.

Very crowded in summer — its main drawback. Difficult to find spots from noon onward during peak season. Water is clear in the morning, becomes murky in the afternoon with the crowds. Visite château · résidence Amédée VIII et Félix V
Visite combinée avec le Musée-Château
Visite commentée du musée et accès au clocher de la fonderie, tous les jours sur réservation. Tarif 13 € adulte, 9 € enfant, durée environ 1 h 30.
Visite de la chartreuse de Ripaille fondée par Amédée VIII en 1410, bâtiments actuels XVIIe-XVIIIe. Cellules des chartreux, cloître, église conventuelle. Vie monastique recluse jusqu'à la Révolution.

Visite des 4 logis du Château d'XQV0Z retraçant l'évolution résidentielle princière : Logis Vieux (XIIIe-XIVe), Logis Perrière (fin XIVe), Logis Nemours (~1535), Logis Neuf (XVIe-XVIIe). Architecture médiévale et Renaissance.

Visite des appartements du XQV0Z : résidence du duc Amédée VIII de Savoie (devenu pape Félix V) qui s'y retira en 1434 avec 5 chevaliers de l'Ordre de Saint-Maurice. Salle des chevaliers, chapelle, mobilier Belle Époque Engel-Gros.
Visite des cellules et salle de torture
Visite des gorges impossible en raison de l'étroitesse des passerelles et des escaliers ; accès possible uniquement jusqu'à la billetterie par le sentier goudronné depuis le parking N°1.
Visite du XQV0Z : maison forte XIIIe-XIVe siècle fortifiée par le comte de Savoie, inscrite Monument Historique 1974 (POP PA00118354). Architecture défensive savoyarde typique conservée et restaurée.
Visite du XQV0Z : maison forte XIVe siècle, l'un des très rares bâtiments médiévaux conservés à XQV1Z. Épargnée par la Révolution française ET par l'incendie de 1840 qui détruisit la vieille ville.

Visite du cabinet de curiosités du XQV0Z : collection de 1500+ pièces rassemblées par Léon Marès - armures samouraï japonaises XVIIe, faïences, armes anciennes africaines et asiatiques, bronzes, manuscrits, ivoires, objets ethnographiques.
Visite guidée du Muséum par un membre de l'association Les Amis du Muséum sur réservation au 07 78 41 33 51. Durée environ 1h30, 15 € forfait. Idéal pour les groupes et passionnés d'entomologie.

Visite guidée hebdomadaire 'Viuz-Faverges, 2500 ans d'histoire' tous les Mardis à 17h en juillet-août. Journées Européennes du Patrimoine (mi-septembre, entrée souvent gratuite). Programmation et conférences sur archeoviuz.fr et echosciences-savoie-mont-blanc.fr.
Visite guidée hebdomadaire pendant juillet-août : adulte 5 €, enfant 5-14 ans 3,50 €. Parcours commenté de 2500 ans d'histoire locale depuis le Néolithique jusqu'à l'époque médiévale.

Well rooted in XQV0Z local life, the cinema acts as a cultural hub for the Pays du XQV1Z: mainstream programming, holiday animations and partnerships with schools (École et cinéma, Collège au cinéma). The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.

XQV0Z is accessible via several hiking routes, with the main starting points at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce (Plateau de la Croix), Bettex via Les Communailles, Planay hamlet from XQV1Z, or Baptieu hamlet from XQV2Z. The climb is challenging and sustained, crossing alpine meadows and forests before reaching the mineral ridges of the summit. Hikers walk among summer grazing herds (Tarine and XQV3Z cows, goats and sheep) whose milk is used to make Beaufort cheese. The final section, particularly exposed from the XQV4Z refuge onward, becomes more technical with steep and rocky passages with sparse markings, requiring great caution.

XQV0Z is accessible via several routes. The departure from Plateau de la Croix (Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce) is the most direct: 4 km, 1,000 m elevation gain, approximately 3 hours ascent. From Bettex (Les Communailles): 5 to 6 hours round trip. From XQV1Z (Planay hamlet): 6 to 8 hours round trip.

XQV0Z is accessible year-round. Spring (April–May) and summer (June–September) are the best seasons to admire the emerald colour of the water and forest flowering. In summer, the site is very busy; prefer weekday mornings for more tranquillity. Free shuttles run from 14 June to 14 September 2025. In autumn (October), foliage turns to brilliant colours and visitor numbers drop. In winter (December–March), the lake is often frozen and snow-covered; the trail remains accessible by snowshoe from Plaine-Joux (ski station open). The light on XQV1Z is particularly beautiful in late afternoon year-round.

In July–August, proximity to La Crique beach (same site) allows combining playground and swimming in the same half-day. Bring sun cream for workshops at forest edges, and dress appropriately for weather (no swimwear in the playground). Special events (Easter, Halloween, autumn festival) require advance booking.

XQV0Z is open in summer (June 15 – Sept 15) daily from 10am to 7pm, and in winter (Jan–Mar) Monday to Friday from 2pm to 6pm. Spring and autumn: afternoon opening except Saturday. Verified on the official website on May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

XQV0Z is open two seasons per year: winter (December to April) for skiers and holidaymakers, and summer (June to August) for hikers and mountain bikers. No reservation is required (except for groups). Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult (18 years and over). The centre also offers complementary activities: aquafitness classes (Mon-Fri 10:30 am, 45 min + 15 min hot tub), private swimming lessons, Avoriaz Quest underwater escape game, and scuba diving initiation.

XQV0Z is open two seasons: in winter (December to mid-April) for skiers and holidaymakers, and in summer (late June to late August) for hikers and mountain bikers. In winter, the centre is very busy during school holidays and weekends (check the Affluences app to avoid crowds). In summer, the atmosphere is quieter and Avoriaz resort is often empty, making for a more intimate experience. The outdoor hot tub is particularly popular in winter after a day of skiing, with views of snow-capped peaks. Aquafitness sessions at 10:30 am allow you to enjoy the pools in the morning before afternoon crowds. In season, last entry at 6:45 pm, pool evacuation at 7:15 pm.

XQV0Z is open year-round with varying hours. In winter (Dec–Mar): Wed/Sat/Sun 1 pm–5 pm, school holidays daily 11 am–5 pm. In season (Apr–Aug): Mon–Fri 1 pm–5:30 pm, weekends 10 am–7:30 pm. Verified on official website May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
XQV0Z is open year-round, 7 days/week (except 25 Dec and 1 Jan). Extended hours in summer (9am-7pm). Booking recommended for groups and high season (July-August). Balaclava mandatory.

XQV0Z is open year-round, with a two-week annual closure during year-end holidays. The Wellness area and thermal cures operate Monday to Saturday, with extended weekday hours (until 7pm) allowing visits after work. The Aqua and Fitness areas remain accessible until 9:15pm on weekdays. January through March is the season for conventional cures (cure participants welcome from January 12). Free cures and wellness treatments are available year-round by reservation. Summer offers the advantage of enjoying the Thermes' private garden and outdoor relaxation areas with lake views. Book several days in advance for wellness treatments and aquabike/aquarun classes (limited to 15 participants).

XQV0Z is open year-round. Free access 1 January to 30 June and 1 September to 31 December. Paid access with lifeguard supervision 1 July to 31 August from 9:30 to 17:30. Closed 7:00 to 9:30 for cleaning in July–August. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

XQV0Z is reached from the Petit Port car park in XQV1Z-le-Vieux (free) via the Bois Jettaz trail and the GR Tour du Lac towards Pré Vernet, then Col des Sauts and the ridge trail. Allow 2 h 30–3 h round trip. Vehicle access to Col des Contrebandiers has been prohibited since July 2024.
XQV0Z is set in the heart of Parc des Dronières, route des Dronières, 74350 XQV1Z, halfway between XQV2Z and XQV3Z (~20 km from each).

XQV0Z majoritairement accessible PMR : ~80% en voies vertes site propre avec enrobé lisse. Dénivelé modéré (~150 m sur 40 km). Quelques sections routes partagées vélos roulants. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.
XQV0Z museum in a 1705 house listed as a Monument Historique. Traditional alpine architecture, open July–August. Adult €5, free under 18.

XQV0Z ne prend pas de réservation. La billetterie se fait sur place uniquement aux horaires d'ouverture, paiement en CB ou espèces (chèques et chèques vacances refusés). Le parc n'accueille pas les groupes scolaires.

XQV0Z offers 4 daily shows in the Eagle Stadium (1,000 seats with 400 covered). Lords of the Sky at 11:00 and 15:30: grand show with 80 raptors in free flight. Birds of the Lake at 14:00 and 17:00: fishing and diving demonstrations of aquatic raptors. All shows are included in the admission price.
XQV0Z offers a free view of the Fête du Lac fireworks on the first Saturday of August, by seeking unobstructed viewpoints along the Sentier des Crêtes.

XQV0Z offers several packages: beginner outings for families (from 8-10 years depending on the canyon), sporty routes for fit adults, fun aquatic canyons for groups of friends or stag/hen parties, technical full-day outings for confirmed practitioners. Full gear is included in the price (5 mm neoprene wetsuit, helmet, harness, boots); you only need to bring swimwear, a towel and closed shoes that can get wet. Groups are deliberately kept small (4-8 people per instructor) for safety and quality.
XQV0Z offre des tyroliennes spectaculaires jusqu'à 400 m de longueur au-dessus du Parc Thermal de Saint-Gervais. Vue dégagée sur le massif du XQV1Z - cadre paysager unique en accrobranche.
XQV0Z opening hours vary by season: see kartingdumontblanc.com/horaires. In case of uncertain weather, contact before coming. Verified on June 5, 2026.

XQV0Z opens in June on weekends 11am to 6pm, in July-August daily 10am to 7pm, in spring and autumn Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays afternoon, and daily afternoons during school holidays. The park is closed in April and May 2026. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

XQV0Z operates only during summer season, from 1 July to 31 August. Best conditions occur from mid-July to mid-August, when the park is open continuously and stream flow is optimal for canyoning. In early and late season, some activities may only be accessible by reservation (outside continuous opening). The park offers a cool, shaded setting ideal during hot summer days. Canyoning is subject to weather conditions and water flow — in case of heavy rain or storms, certain activities may be suspended for safety reasons.

XQV0Z ouvre 7j/7 toute l'année. Lun 14h-19h, Mar 14h-20h, Mer 10h-12h30 et 14h-21h, Jeu 14h-22h15, Ven 14h-23h30, Sam 10h-12h30 et 14h-23h30, Dim 10h-12h30 et 14h-19h. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

XQV0Z ouvre de Pâques à la Toussaint. Juillet-août tous les jours 10h–19h, avril-juin et septembre-octobre week-ends + vacances scolaires zone A. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières et météo.

XQV0Z ouvre de mai à octobre. Juillet-août tous les jours 9h–18h30. Mai-juin et septembre-octobre week-ends et jours fériés 14h–18h. Fermé du 1er novembre au 30 avril. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : cour intérieure Renaissance et rez-de-chaussée accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages par escalier uniquement (pas d'ascenseur). Tarif gratuit pour PMR + accompagnant.
XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : cour intérieure Renaissance et rez-de-chaussée accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages par escalier uniquement (pas d'ascenseur). Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : cour intérieure et douves visibles depuis le pont-levis accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages château et donjon NON accessibles (escaliers étroits médiévaux). Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : cour intérieure et douves visibles depuis le pont-levis accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages château et donjon non accessibles (escaliers étroits médiévaux). Tarif gratuit -5 ans.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : cour intérieure, arboretum 53 ha, salle des chevaliers (rez-de-chaussée) accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages château et chartreuse NON accessibles. Tarif handicap réduit 5 €. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : cour intérieure, arboretum 53 ha, salle des chevaliers (rez-de-chaussée) accessibles fauteuils roulants. Étages château et chartreuse non accessibles. Tarif handicap réduit 5 €.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : rez-de-chaussée du château et parc paysager 4 ha accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Donjon cylindrique NON accessible PMR (escalier en colimaçon). Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.
XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : rez-de-chaussée du château et parc paysager 4 ha accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Le donjon cylindrique n'est pas accessible PMR (escalier en colimaçon).

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : rez-de-chaussée et entresol accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages par escalier en pierre uniquement (pas d'ascenseur). Tarif réduit 4 € pour situations de handicap.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : rez-de-chaussée et entresol accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages par escalier en pierre uniquement. Tarif réduit 4 € pour situations de handicap. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : voies vertes en site propre (~60% du tracé) accessibles aux fauteuils roulants et poussettes. Routes partagées (~40%) non garanties PMR. Étape XQV1Z-XQV2Z 16 km entièrement PMR. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.
XQV0Z prices: see kartingdumontblanc.com/tarifs. Payment accepted: cash, credit card, ANCV holiday vouchers (June 1 - August 25). Verified on June 5, 2026.
XQV0Z propose en complément une via ferrata sécurisée pour grimpeurs débutants à confirmés. Site multi-activités outdoor unique en Pays du XQV1Z - alternative ou complément aux activités glaciaires.

XQV0Z propose plus de 80 expériences VR au catalogue, régulièrement renouvelé : jeux d'action, escape games VR, simulateurs sportifs et de pilotage, expériences artistiques et culturelles. Sur stations individuelles de 9 m².

XQV0Z propose un snack salé sur place entre 11h et 13h (prise de commande jusqu'à 12h45). Les aliments et boissons extérieurs sont interdits, sauf aliments spécifiques bébé, biberons (micro-ondes à disposition), eau et fruits.

XQV0Z présente 35 sculptures grandeur nature de dinosaures et créatures préhistoriques : T-Rex, vélociraptors, stégosaures, tricératops, dimétrodons, spinosaures, ptérosaures, du bébé dino aux adultes imposants sur 1,5 hectare de jungle.
XQV0Z rates are not publicly displayed. To learn the prices for courses, ziplines, canyoning and combined packages, contact the park directly at 06 83 19 21 18. Verified 14 May 2026.

XQV0Z se vit de mars à octobre, fenêtre où le parc est ouvert le mercredi, samedi et dimanche, plus le mardi pendant les vacances scolaires zone A (Pâques, Toussaint, février, Noël). L'été (vacances scolaires d'été) ouvre du mercredi au dimanche jusqu'à 18 h. La jungle est dense et ombragée, idéale par temps chaud ; en revanche, les allées de terre deviennent boueuses par temps humide — prévoir bottes et vêtements adaptés. La dernière entrée est 1 h avant la fermeture. Le 20e anniversaire en 2026 peut donner lieu à des animations spéciales — à vérifier sur le site officiel.

XQV0Z se vit toute l'année et la fréquentation suit un rythme prévisible. Les mercredis après-midi, samedis, dimanches et vacances scolaires zone A sont les pics — arriver avant 11 h ou après 17 h pour éviter l'affluence aquatique. Les matins de semaine hors vacances (12 h–15 h, bassin natation seul) sont les créneaux les plus calmes et les moins chers (adulte 10 €). L'espace aquatique extérieur ouvre par saison (ex. 23-25 mai 2026 en haute saison anticipée, puis fenêtre estivale juin-septembre) — c'est l'argument principal pour venir en été. Le Spa est l'option d'hiver par excellence, particulièrement le soir après le travail.
XQV0Z town hall: +33 (0)4 50 44 14 60 · Monts du Genevois TO: +33 (0)4 50 04 71 63

XQV0Z treetop courses range from €6 (Pitchounes, 10 games) to €26 (Everest, 96 games). Intermediate prices: Little hikers €11, Big hikers €14, Climbers €17, Alpinists €23. Options: airbag €2/jump, catapult €10/2 jumps, escape game €68 (4 people). Verified 14 May 2026.

XQV0Z winter rate for adults is €12. Child 3-11 years: €8. Senior 65+: €11. Summer adult rate: €11. Free under 2 years. Free entry with the Avoriaz MultiPass, -10% with Alpine Express. Verified 6 June 2026.
XQV0Z · Dog sledding in XQV1Z (Portes du Soleil)
XQV0Z · Thonon · FREE · Porte des Lombards
XQV0Z · Thonon · €12 · Residence of Amadeus VIII
XQV0Z · XQV1Z (74) | Loisirs 74

XQV0Z à Saint-Gervais propose 7 parcours accrobranche de niveaux progressifs : parcours enfants (à partir de 3 ans), parcours initiation, intermédiaire, sportif, expert. Atelier découverte sécurité au démarrage obligatoire.
XQV0Z été

XQV0Z été : adulte 15 € 'Malin' (J-5) ou 18 € 'Liberté' ; enfant 3–11 ans 12 € / 14 €. Pack 2 parcs adulte 25 €. Grand Parc de Noël adulte 20 €. Médiévales 20 € adulte. Moins de 3 ans gratuit. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

XQV0Z, 74000 — see la-grande-evasion.com/annecy

Y a-t-il des visites guidées à la XQV0Z d'XQV1Z ?
Y a-t-il un Pass Musées à l'XQV0Z ?
Y a-t-il un Pass Musées à la XQV0Z ?

Y a-t-il une offre fidélité à la XQV0Z ?
Year-round subject to snow conditions. Road not snow-cleared in winter from Grotte du Diable (1.5 km before pass). Occasional closure on July 7, 2026 (9am-12pm) for cycling event XQV0Z Au Sommet.
Year-round · Summer lifeguard supervision from start of school holidays to August 31, 11 a.m.–7 p.m.

Year-round, daily. Supervised swimming July–August from 12pm to 6pm. WARNING: site undergoing refurbishment September 2025 to June 2026 — access to swimming and fishing lakes prohibited during this period; northern lake (windsurfing) and Arve embankment remain accessible.
Year-round, free access. Supervised swimming July 1 to August 31, 10:30 am–7:00 pm. Verified on Thonon city website May 14, 2026.
Year-round, free access. Supervised swimming July 6 to September 1, 12pm–6pm.
Yes, Le Buet station car park is the official parking: 2 h free, then €3 for 5 h or €5/day. Very limited in high season — prefer the XQV0Z Express train (Le Buet stop).
Yes, Les Planards car park: 200 spaces (around 800 in summer). Rate €12/day. Free Biollay car park 5 min walk away. Shuttle bus line 14 (winter) or walk from town centre (5 min).
Yes, XQV0Z is supervised July 1 to August 31, daily from 10:30 am to 7:00 pm, with a rescue post and lifeguards on duty. Outside this period, swimming is not supervised.
Yes, a free shuttle links XQV0Z (village and Chinaillon) to the XQV1Z from 11 July to 21 August 2026. Regular departures all day. Convenient for hikers and cyclists doing one-way trips.
Yes, a large free car park at the resort of XQV0Z, split between P1 Nordic and P2 Alpine. In summer, the road continues up to the Crêt de Châtillon; in winter, parking at the resort is mandatory.
Yes, but MTB access is suspended from 24 August 17:30 until end of season. Pedestrian trips remain available. Spectators can ride the gondola to watch the racing.

Yes, for children aged 8–10 and up who are accustomed to mountain hiking. The Flégère route is marked and secure but includes technical passages (metal ladders) and 570 m elevation gain. Bring hiking boots, water, and snacks.
Yes, from 13 June 2026. The Brévent Cable Car opens for hikers with access to the Grand Balcon Sud, the Brévent–Flégère traverse and Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve routes.
Yes, in season. Entry to the Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum includes a visit to the Manoir de la Tour (open in summer), an emblematic medieval residence in the historic centre of XQV1Z.
Yes, on arrival of the Débaroule at the Bossonnet you can continue with the Luge des Bois — another flagship summer attraction in XQV0Z, also included in the Pass Découverte.

Yes, partially. XQV0Z has trails partly accessible to people with reduced mobility. Vehicle access is possible to the La Châtaignière domain. Some lakeside trails are natural and not passable by vehicle.
Yes, racket rental (padel, tennis, squash) is usually available at the club for around €3-5 per session. Balls are sold individually or by tube. Bring sportswear and clean indoor shoes.

Yes, restaurant À la Belle Étoile and snack bar 'la case à Caza' are located at Beunaz Beach. You can have lunch, a snack or refreshments between climbing and swimming. Picnic areas are also available.

Yes, several hotels and restaurants at the summit: Les Rosières (family Savoyard cuisine), La Table de Marie-Ange (gourmet, 2 Gault & Millau toques), Les Sapins. All offer panoramic terraces. Some open year-round.

Yes, several trails leave from the col: Tête des Annes (2 h round trip, 560 m climb), Refuge de Doran (3 h round trip, 780 m climb), Lac de Peyre (3 h 30 round trip). Yellow waymarking, intermediate to confirmed level. IGN map 3430ET recommended.

Yes, the Angon canyon is a premier canyoning destination near XQV0Z. Eight waterfalls total, with main XQV1Z (60 m rappel) as the finale. Accessible with professional guided instruction. Practiced in summer; Ice Canyoning possible in winter with appropriate gear.

Yes, the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is part of a collection of four routes in the Chamonix Valley: XQV1Z, XQV2Z, Les Bois de Chamonix and Argentière. You can chain the routes using the XQV3Z Express.

Yes, the Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard, at the end of the route, offers drinks, snacks, croque-monsieur and homemade tarts on a terrace facing the waterfall (open in season, generally 10am-6pm weather permitting, mainly cash payment).
Yes, the Casino of XQV0Z has three restaurants: Le Safran (gastronomic), Le Paradise (bar-brasserie) and Le Maloja (restaurant). Booking recommended, especially for Le Safran at weekends.
Yes, the Lac du Môle loop has been adapted to be accessible to people with reduced mobility and to prams — a rare path in XQV0Z suitable for all ages. Checked on 10 June 2026.
Yes, the Secrets de fées trails are passable in winter with snowshoes, depending on weather conditions and snow cover on the Plateau des Moises. Check with the Foyer des Moises before setting off.

Yes, the cinema regularly programmes films for young audiences (Wednesday, Saturday, school holidays) as well as specific operations (Ciné-goûter, École et cinéma, holiday events). The young-audience programme is published each week.
Yes, three escape routes allow you to exit after each section: after Le Cabri, after Le Chamois (Le Vionnet exit) and after Le Bouquetin. You can thus adapt the route to your fitness.
Yes, two 150-meter zip-lines soar above the waterfall at 40 meters height at XQV0Z. Minimum weight required: 30 kg. Zip-lines cost extra beyond the cave tour admission (inquire on-site).

Yes. Alpine Coaster from age 3 accompanied by an adult, solo from 1.35 m. Amusement park: rides from age 1 (carousels, diggers), mini-karts and slides from age 3, trampolines from age 4, splash boat and loops from age 7. Free playground on site.
Yes. Animals are allowed at the Parc des Dronières and on the Dronières Lake loop. Follow standard rules: leash recommended, clean up after your dog, and keep clear of the pool area in season.

Yes. Refurbishment works at the XQV0Z are underway from September 2025 to June 2026. During this period, paths around the fishing and swimming lakes are closed, as are the north and central car parks. The northern lake (windsurfing) and the Arve embankment remain accessible. For your safety, respect barriers in place.
Yes. Several paragliding schools operate from Plaine-Joux: Aerofiz, Élévation Parapente, De Cimes en Ciel. Tandem flights with qualified instructors, year-round by reservation.

Yes. Several trails start from the XQV0Z, including the Croix de Fer and chalet du Curé loop on the La Giettaz side, and the climb to the Porte des Aravis. Check with the tourist office for conditions and waymarking.
Yes. Swimming at XQV0Z is supervised by lifeguards from 1 July to 30 August, daily from 11:15 AM to 5:45 PM. Outside this period, swimming is free but unsupervised.
Yes. Swimming is supervised in one of the two lakes at XQV0Z from July 6 to September 1, 12pm to 6pm. Outside the supervision period, swimming is possible but unsupervised.

Yes. The Dronières Lake is classified as a closed water and managed by the association Les Eaux Closes. A specific permit is required (€13/day, €80/year in 2025). The French federal FNPF / AAPPMA card is not valid here. Permits are sold at La Fleuristerie and Gamm Vert in XQV0Z, or online via HelloAsso.

Yes. The Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z is open all winter from mid-December to end of October, every day 2 pm-7 pm (except Saturdays from 1 January to 30 April). A perfect activity after a day on the slopes.
Yes. The Thiou and its quays are accessible year-round, at any time. The main attractions (Palais de l'Île, Musée-Château) follow their own opening hours.
Yes. The XQV0Z (Pont Charles-Albert) has been listed as a Historic Monument by decree of 6 May 1966. It is owned by the XQV1Z Departmental Council.

Yes. The XQV0Z and the Château de Montrottier are linked by the Chemin des Châteaux, a free 2 km walking loop with 13 interpretation panels. The commune of XQV1Z has developed this route to make combining the two sites easy.
Yes. The combined ticket at €7 adult / €5 child covers the Wildlife Museum AND XQV0Z's other museum (Brevon Valley History Museum). Excellent value.
Yes. The end point of the Pointe Percée Geological Trail is the Pointe Percée refuge (FFCAM, ~2164 m), staffed in season, where you can stock up on a drink or meal according to the opening calendar.
Yes. Two bars-restaurants are present at XQV0Z: L'Entre 2 Lacs and the Espace Le Bois aux Dames bar. Quick service, snacks, drinks. Terraces with lake views.
Yes. Two free car parks allow walking access to the XQV0Z: the Bété car park and the Morsulaz car park, both in XQV1Z. Motor traffic beyond these points is prohibited.
Yes. XQV0Z has free on-site parking, directly accessible from the route de la Plage (locality La Beunaz).
Yes. XQV0Z has free parking on-site at 409 route du Moulin de la Glacière in XQV1Z, in the Coudrée Estate.

Yes. XQV0Z is located at Beunaz Beach, which offers supervised swimming, paddle, canoe, pedalo, inflatable lake structures, Télébeune (zip line), swimming pool, diving boards, restaurant and snack bar. Perfect for a full day.
You can reach the start of the Eterlou Trail by car to the Pré-la-Joux car park, or use XQV0Z's summer shuttle Line 1, stop Pré-la-Joux.

departs from the heart of XQV0Z village (1,000 m) and climbs to the Pléney summit, opening up a wide view over the Chablais, Lake Geneva and the Mont Blanc range. It is the most central lift in XQV1Z, located on Route du Téléphérique steps from the village shops.

enjoy Lake Geneva in a unique setting — the XQV0Z opening onto the Swiss shore, Évian's belle-époque promenade, the panorama of the Vaud mountains — at affordable group rates (paddle from €15/h, kayak €20/h, supervised sessions around €25/session).

for temporary shows ('Wildwood, LAIKA', 21 June-27 September 2026), a

for understanding mountain environments (snow conditions in winter, judging conditions, choosing an objective). Introduction courses: mountaineering, ski touring, multi-pitch climbing. Prices from around €60-100 per person for a half-day group outing, more for private outings and high-mountain ascents. Booking ahead is essential, especially in high season (July-August, February, weekends).
