is Chamonix valley's most technical ski area, above the village of Argentière. It spans 1,235 m to 2,800 m (summit Aiguille des Grands Montets at 3,275 m) and is world-renowned for black pistes and off-piste terrain beside the Argentière glacier. Since the August 2018 fire that destroyed the original Lognan cable cars, access has been via the Plan Joran gondola (2,134 m), with skiers only permitted on board.

is a 15-hectare mountain lake at 1080 m altitude in the hamlet of La Chèvrerie, in the commune of XQV0Z. It formed on the night of 11–12 March 1943, when a mudslide swept away about ten chalets and blocked the course of the Brévon river. Water accumulated behind this natural dam, submerging the hamlet of L'Écondu, whose foundations can still sometimes be seen beneath the surface.

is a 6-person lift operated by SOLEGETS at XQV0Z (74260). It connects the village base (1,170 m) to the Chavannes plateau (1,482 m) in 5 minutes over 1,545 m, with a 312 m vertical rise and a throughput of 1,200 people per hour. It serves as the main entry point to the XQV1Z Bike Park in summer.

is a 6-person lift operated by SOLEGETS at XQV0Z (74260). It connects the village centre (1,162 m) to the Mont-Chéry summit (1,535 m) in 5 minutes, with a 373 m vertical rise and a throughput of about 1,200 people per hour. Built in 1981 and upgraded, it allows both uphill and downhill passenger travel — less common than gondolas that only carry guests uphill. The lower station sits 200 m from the village centre, next to the main parking areas.

is a Pre-Alps mountain in XQV0Z but belongs geologically to the Jura chain. Col des Pitons is particularly popular with cyclists: six climbing routes are mapped, from easiest (XQV1Z, 7.5 km at 4.8% average gradient) to most challenging (XQV2Z, 11.7 km at 7% average with sections exceeding 12%). For hikers, the pass serves as a starting point or waypoint toward Grand Piton, Thuile alpine pasture, or various trails on the GR Balcon du XQV3Z. At the Grand Piton summit, an orientation table helps identify Alpine and Jura peaks, while an engraved cartouche recalls the passage of Lord Byron and Alphonse de Lamartine, author of a famous poem dedicated to Le Salève.

is a concrete panoramic platform situated at 1,254 m altitude on the ridge overlooking XQV0Z and Lake XQV1Z. It is the sole remaining vestige of the former Mont Veyrier cable car, inaugurated on 29 July 1934, decommissioned in 1984 due to the cost of safety upgrades, with its upper station demolished in 2001 following a fatal accident in 1998. At its peak in 1948, this cable car transported nearly 70,000 passengers per year in just 7 minutes from XQV2Z, with two 25-passenger cabins, setting two world records: the longest cable span without intermediate pylons (1,525 m) and the greatest elevation gain (808 m).

is a forest adventure park located at Beunaz Beach, at 900 metres altitude. The park offers 7 treetop courses of increasing difficulty, with continuous safety lines for maximum security. The activity is accessible from age 2 (Little Kid course under adult supervision), with courses adapted by height: Little Kid (age 2 to 105 cm), Kid (105 cm to 135 cm), and Indiana Park (from 135 cm, with blue, red and black courses).

is a former thermal establishment built in 1902 by architect Ernest Brunnarius, located on quai Charles-Albert Besson, facing Lake XQV0Z. A historical monument listed in 1986, the building was purchased in 1996 by the City of Évian and completely renovated by architects Michel Spitz and François Châtillon to reopen in 2006 as a cultural and congress center.

is a glacial alpine lake located at 1,358 meters in the municipality of XQV0Z, at the foot of the Aravis range. Formed over 10,000 years ago during the last ice age, this 3-hectare body of water reaches a depth of 6 meters. Accessible by road from the village of XQV1Z (5 km), it lies on an open alpine plateau between Mont Danay and the Aravis combes.

is a historic castle located in XQV0Z (formerly XQV1Z), in the XQV2Z department. Built in 1060 on the orders of Count Gérold of Geneva, this medieval fortress has been inhabited by the de Sales family since 1559. Today, it operates as a 4-star hotel and reception venue, managed by the de Roussy de Sales family.

is a limestone plateau located in the Bornes massif, at an average altitude of 1,450 m, extending across the communes of Glières-Val-de-Borne and XQV0Z. Accessible from XQV1Z via a winding 15 km road, the plateau alternates between alpine pastures, peat bogs, coniferous forests and rocky ridges. It is framed by Montagne des Auges (1,822 m) to the east and Montagne des Frêtes (1,910 m) to the northwest.

is a medieval fortress transformed into a stately residence, dominating Lake XQV0Z from a rocky spur 200 metres above the water. Built from the 12th century onwards, it comprises today 105 rooms spread throughout an architectural ensemble blending medieval elements (three original square towers), Renaissance transformations, and 19th-century Romantic embellishments under the impetus of René de Menthon, great-grandfather of the current owners.

is a natural glacial lake in XQV0Z located at 1,200 metres altitude in the municipality of XQV1Z, in the heart of a coniferous forest. It covers approximately ten hectares and owes its name to the characteristic emerald colour of its waters, caused by subaquatic vegetation and the presence of cyanobacteria. A natural heritage site classified on 14 June 1909, it offers a remarkable panorama of the XQV2Z massif and the Fiz mountain range.

is a natural waterfall 70 meters high located at approximately 1,200 m elevation in the municipality of XQV0Z. It has been classified as a Natural Monument since June 14, 1909, and is protected under XQV1Z's natural heritage designation. Visible from the floor of the Arve valley (notably from the motorway or national road toward Chamonix), it stands out in the middle of a broadleaf forest, perched 600 meters above the plain.

is a protected natural site of 85 hectares located in the municipality of XQV0Z, on the shores of Lake Geneva. Classified as Natura 2000, it is owned by the Conservatoire du Littoral et des Rivages Lacustres and is managed collectively by the municipality of XQV1Z, LPO Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, the ONF, and the Guidou Estate Management Association. The site comprises a mosaic of habitats: wooded areas, cereal crop fields, wet and dry meadows, and reed beds.

is a thermal and wellness establishment located at Place de la Libération in XQV0Z, on the shores of Lake XQV1Z. Founded historically in 1827 following the discovery in 1789 by Count Jean-Charles de Laizer of the therapeutic properties of evian water, the thermal baths today offer three activity areas: an Aqua space (conventional and free thermal cures), a Wellness pole (aesthetic treatments and massages), and a Fitness area (group classes, cardio-strength training, aqua fitness).

is a very easy interpretive route waymarked by the XQV0Z Tourist Office in the Vallée d'XQV1Z, within the Portes du Soleil ski area. About 3.5 km long, it takes around 2 h 30 to walk, climbing gently through the Vorraz, Louennaz and Sur l'Ortaz pastures from the Route du Petit-XQV2Z up to Super-XQV3Z.
is also open on the adjacent beach from June to September (12 pm–6 pm, 11 am–7 pm in July–August).

is at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce, a high-altitude hamlet (≈1,170 m) of XQV0Z in the Mont Blanc area. It occupies the 18th-c. former presbytery, next to the remarkable baroque Saint-Nicolas church (Historic Monument). Opened in December 2010, it highlights the baroque sacred-art treasure of the parish, one of the richest in the diocese of XQV1Z.
is currently being prepared by the commune of XQV0Z and the Communauté de Communes XQV1Z-Glières: way-marking, interpretation panels, securing of the ruins, schedule still to be confirmed.

is the highest road pass on the Le Salève massif, located at 1,335 m elevation on the D41A, between Col de la Croisette (1,176 m) and XQV0Z. It sits at the foot of Grand Piton (1,379 m), the massif's highest point, crowned by the Bastian Tower. This historic tower, built between 1820 and 1830 by Claude-François Bastian, a notary and mayor of Frangy, stands on the site of a 14th-century lookout post. The pass is accessible by car, bike, and on foot, with dedicated parking on-site.

is the inter-municipal water complex of the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'XQV0Z community. Set just steps from Lake Geneva, it offers more than 4,500 m² of facilities: a 25m six-lane sports pool, a fun pool, a paddling pool for toddlers, slides, a current river and a wellness area with sauna and hammam. It is one of the main indoor aquatic facilities of the Évian area, used by both locals and holidaymakers.

is the northernmost mountain of the Bauges massif. At 15 km long, the plateau overlooks the western shore of Lake XQV0Z from the Col de Leschaux in the south to the city of XQV1Z in the north. Its highest point, the Crêt de Châtillon, reaches 1699 m and features an orientation table offering a 360° view over Mont Blanc, XQV2Znette, the Aravis, the Bauges, Lake XQV3Z and, in clear weather, Lake Bourget.

is the only large natural sand beach on Lake XQV0Z. Located on the west shore facing Dents de Lanfon and Tournette, it spans approximately 2,200 m² and combines fine sand beach (approx. 100 metres long) with vast shaded lawns. The municipal beach is open year-round: free access September to June, paid and lifeguarded access 1 July to 31 August from 9:30 to 17:30.

is the only natural fine sand beach on the French shore of Lake Geneva. Located in Coudrée Bay, about ten kilometres from XQV0Z and 25 km from Geneva, it represents a remarkable geological exception: its sand comes from sub-glacial deposits formed after the last ice age (12,000 to 15,000 years ago), reworked by violent winds and Rhône currents. The lake dunes thus created are unique in Europe.

of XQV0Z, better known as the Muséâm or Bernard Lacroix Museum, sits in an old 18th-c. family farm originally built by the Carthusians of XQV1Z. It holds the largest collection of rural objects in XQV2Z: over

on the second cable. The panorama covers the Portes du Soleil peaks, the XQV0Z valley and, on clear days, the Mont Blanc massif in the background. Each ride lasts about a minute and a half — plan 1 h 30 on site with briefing, lift access and both descents.

outings around Lake XQV0Z and in the Bauges, Bornes and Aravis ranges. The team organises guided descents led by state-qualified instructors (BEES or DEJEPS canyon) in several iconic canyons of the area — Angon, Montmin, Ternèze, Chéran and around, depending on water levels and the experience sought. The activity combines abseiling, jumps, natural slides and whitewater swimming in spectacular Alpine scenery just a few kilometres from XQV1Z.

overlook the village of XQV0Z in the Vallée Verte, 25 km south-east of XQV1Z. The château's origins go back to the 15th century, when the marquis of XQV2Z built a first Gothic-style castle; further wings were added until the 17th century, while preserving the medieval silhouette. The seigneury passed in the early 14th century to the nobles of Fernex, returned in 1429 to Thomas de Genève-XQV3Z, and was bought in 1696 by the Gerbais de Sonnaz family.

serves the Brévent ski area at 2,525 m, south-facing and directly opposite the Mont Blanc massif above Chamonix. Access from the town centre uses two sections: a gondola to Planpraz (2,000 m) then the cable car to the Brévent summit. In summer (13 June – 20 September 2026, excluding partial closures), the site opens to hikers for sightseeing, with routes to Planpraz, the Grand Balcon Sud traverse and the Brévent–Flégère link.

À XQV0Z : parc aquatique 25 m + 9 toboggans + balnéo, spa romano-irlandais, salle d'escalade 14 m, plaine de jeux indoor enfants, squash/badminton/fitness, restaurants et galerie commerciale, hôtel sur site.

À XQV0Z, la XQV1Z (2231 route du Mont) est le site principal CCVCMB sur l'agriculture de montagne. La Maison de l'Alpage (113 rue du Bouchet) est un site distinct, plus petit, focalisé sur le travail en alpage. Les deux sont gratuits.
À XQV0Z, la XQV1Z se raccorde à la Véloroute V61 XQV2Z-XQV3Z pour rejoindre Chamonix (50 km côté 74) ou redescendre vers XQV4Z-XQV5Z. Réseau cyclable complet vallée de l'Arve.
À XQV0Z, la XQV1Z se raccorde à la Véloroute V61 XQV2Z-XQV3Z pour rejoindre Chamonix (50 km côté 74). Réseau cyclable complet vallée de l'Arve - XQV4Z accessible à vélo.
À XQV0Z, la XQV1Z se raccorde à la XQV2Z EV17 EuroVelo et permet la continuité vers le XQV3Z au nord (140 km vers XQV4Z) ou Chamonix-Argentière au sud (prolongement 2025-2030).
À XQV0Z, la XQV1Z se raccorde à la XQV2Z EV17 EuroVelo, axe XQV3Z-Méditerranée. À XQV4Z, raccordement vers Genevois et XQV5Z en cours (travaux 2025-2026).

À la XQV0Z : aire de jeux enfants (portiques, balançoires, toboggans), tables de pique-nique, barbecue en libre accès, jeu de pétanque. Tyrolienne et ping-pong mentionnés mais à vérifier sur place auprès de la mairie.
À ne pas confondre : la Maison de l'Alpage (113 rue du Bouchet, XQV0Z) est un site CCVCMB distinct, plus petit, focalisé sur le travail en alpage. Également gratuit.

Écomusée associatif labellisé depuis 2003 sur le patrimoine paysan du XQV0Z : 500 m² immersifs sur la vie en montagne 1850-1950, plus 7 autres sites.
Église face au musée, point de visite complémentaire libre du village historique des Houches. Architecture savoyarde classique.
Église médiévale adjacente avec crypte archéologique multi-périodes : bâtiment romain 1er siècle, églises 6e, 8e-9e, ~1000, chœur roman 12e, stalles baroques 1696. Visite incluse.
Établissement accessible aux PMR en fauteuil : 10 marches à l'entrée mais rampe fixe extérieure, ascenseur intérieur vers l'étage, places de parking adaptées, sanitaire adapté.
Établissement accessible aux personnes en fauteuil roulant : ascenseur, toilettes adaptées, places de parking adaptées à proximité, boucle à induction magnétique.
Établissement entièrement accessible aux PMR ; bassin dédié aux personnes en situation de handicap ; stationnement adapté et réservé, entrée de plain-pied.

Établissement équipé pour les PMR : places de parking dédiées, ascenseurs PMR, cabines de soins PMR et lève-malades ; remises tarifaires sur présentation de la carte PMR.
Étage climat/adaptation de l'Observatoire des Alpes au XQV0Z : changement climatique en haute montagne (recul des glaciers documenté), adaptations vivantes, scénarios prospectifs du massif du XQV1Z.

Étage faune/flore de l'Observatoire des Alpes au XQV0Z : espèces protégées des massifs alpins (gypaète, bouquetin, lagopède), biodiversité étagée (de la forêt aux landes alpines), botanique d'altitude.
Étage géologie/glaciologie de l'Observatoire des Alpes au XQV0Z : formation du massif du XQV1Z, glaciers actifs (Mer de Glace, Argentière), cycles du Quaternaire, érosion glaciaire et torrentielle.

Étage histoire humaine de l'Observatoire des Alpes au XQV0Z : peuplements préhistoriques, alpinisme depuis Saussure (XQV1Z 1786), vie pastorale traditionnelle, transformations contemporaines des sociétés alpines.
Étape 1 classique du XQV0Z : XQV1Z → XQV2Z, 16 km, ~850 m D+, par col de Voza 1 653 m (ou variante plus alpine col de Tricot). Journée de mise en jambes anti-horaire.
Étape 1 de la XQV0Z : 30 km bord du XQV1Z de XQV2Z (frontière franco-suisse) à XQV3Z par XQV4Z. Route partagée + voie verte 2 km entrée Thonon.
Étape 2 XQV0Z : 26 km Thonon → XQV1Z par XQV2Z, XQV3Z (plage de sable), XQV4Z (village médiéval). Bas-Chablais en bord de lac XQV5Z.
Étape XQV0Z → Thonon par Évian
Étape XQV0Z → XQV1Z de la XQV2Z : 16 km ouverts en décembre 2019 avec passerelle sur l'Arve. Vue continue sur le massif du XQV3Z, raccordement avec V61 XQV4Z-XQV5Z.
Étape mythique du XQV0Z : passage par le col de la Tournette à 2 351 m d'altitude (refuge saisonnier de la Tournette à proximité). Vue plongeante sur le lac d'XQV1Z et le massif des Bauges.

— officially the Pont Charles-Albert — is a steel-cable suspension bridge inaugurated on 11 July 1839, straddling the communes of XQV0Z and Allonzier-la-Caille.
